stack_definition.description: Stack initialization means runtime code has explicitly selected a controlled stack region, loaded the stack pointer with that region, proven the pointer is usable for stack-dependent control flow, and documented ownership of that region.

evidence_requirements: KOZO_STACK_INIT_OK marker captured from runtime code
proof_boundary: evidence must come from runtime code, not scripts
evidence must be captured through QEMU serial smoke or a successor governed runtime evidence path
evidence must preserve the existing boot smoke marker sequence until a successor evidence path is accepted
evidence must not replace the halt loop until runtime progression evidence permits it
assumptions_enabled: safe call instruction usage after the proven stack marker
safe function nesting after the proven stack marker
safe runtime progression entry after the proven stack marker
safe progression beyond halt only after separate progression evidence permits halt replacement
